item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents end opposed to giving society automatic priority over individual rights. Agent A starts rights-first and becomes more strongly concerned about emergency powers, weak enforcement, and national-stability claims overriding freedoms. Agent B starts with a more balanced public-health exception frame, then moves closer to Agent A's safeguards/overreach frame while still defending U.S. judicial review and institutional checks. No full flip is visible.
